feedingProfile.preferredNpk: balanced (e.g. 10-10-10) at half strength, growing season only

commonIssues: Overwatering is the main killer — rhizomes and roots rot quickly in wet soil; always let the potting mix dry completely before watering
Direct sun can scorch/scald the glossy leaflets; keep out of full sunlight
Yellowing lower leaves usually signal overwatering, not drought
Watch for scale insects (otherwise few pest or disease problems)
All parts contain calcium oxalate — toxic to cats, dogs, and people if chewed; wash hands after handling
Black spots on stems are normal for this species, not disease
Very slow grower; repot only every 2-3 years in spring, going up one pot size

source.citation: Clemson University HGIC, 'ZZ Plant (Zamioculcas zamiifolia): Indoor Care, Growing Tips & Plant Guide' — water 1-2 times a month only after the potting medium has completely dried; overwatering causes root rot
NC State Extension Plant Toolbox, Zamioculcas zamiifolia — water twice monthly in summer (only if soil has dried out completely) and once a month in winter when not actively growing
Clemson University HGIC — fertilize only once or twice during the growing season with a balanced fertilizer; ~90-day interval encodes this growing-season-only cadence
NC State Extension Plant Toolbox — deep shade (less than 2 hours direct sun) to partial shade (2-6 hours); thrives in bright indirect light; direct rays can scald leaves

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder, Zamioculcas zamiifolia (Kemper code d179) — part shade to full shade; bright indirect light; USDA Zones 9-10; bring indoors when temperatures dip below 60 F (~15.5 C); 2-3 ft tall and wide
Clemson University HGIC — sensitive to cold, keep above 60 F (~15.5 C); coldHardyMinC 15 is the conservative keep-above threshold for container culture, not the absolute survival minimum
Clemson University HGIC — soil mix of 50% peat moss or coir-based potting soil, 25% perlite, 25% sand; good drainage essential, never leave standing water
NC State Extension Plant Toolbox — slow growth rate, 2-4 ft tall and wide; medium toxicity to cats and dogs (calcium oxalate); watch for scale insects

Confidence note: container liters (3 L minimum ≈ 6-inch nursery pot; ideal 4-15 L ≈ 7-12-inch pots) are estimated from the plant's slow growth, mature 2-3 ft size (MoBot/NCSU), and common nursery pot sizes — no extension source states an explicit pot volume

Confidence note: heatStressC 35 and hotWeatherMultiplier 0.9 are conservative estimates — extension sources document cold limits (keep above 60 F) but give no explicit upper heat-stress temperature; ZZ tolerates warm indoor conditions well
